studies.title: Use of a smartphone application (Orthopy App) to improve preoperative and postoperative therapy in patients with ACL rupture compared to conventional standard of care treatment - a randomized, monocentric study.

_verification.reason: The study specifically mentions the 'Orthopy App' as the intervention being tested, which matches the product 'Orthopy bei Knieverletzungen'. The trial registration and full text confirm the use of this app developed by Orthopy Health GmbH.

_classification.reason: The study is described as a 'randomized, monocentric study' comparing the use of a smartphone application to standard care, indicating random allocation to intervention and control groups, which is characteristic of an RCT.

studies.title: Investigation of the effectiveness of integrated care using the Orthopy app in addition to usual care compared to usual care alone in patients with meniscal lesions – a randomized, controlled, multicenter trial

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the effectiveness of the Orthopy app, as indicated by the title and detailed description in the full text. The product name 'Orthopy' and its variations are mentioned multiple times, confirming the study is about this specific digital therapeutic product.

_classification.reason: The study is described as a randomized, controlled, multicenter trial with participants randomly allocated to an intervention group using the Orthopy app in addition to usual care, or a control group receiving usual care alone. Randomization and control conditions are key features of an RCT.

studies.title: Early Postoperative Application of Radial Extracorporeal Shock Wave Therapy (rESWT) and Neuroreflectory Hyberbaric CO2 Cryotherapie Following Primary Reconstruction of the Anterior Cruciate Ligament: A Prospective, Randomized, Controlled Study
